title: airader
description: A session-replay and customer-journey analysis product with public API docs, OpenAPI surfaces, and SEO comparison pages.
body:
airader is the renamed product surface from the recent trakkker repository work: a session-replay tool for watching user sessions, understanding customer-journey friction, and exporting session data into analysis workflows.

The recent commits moved it away from a generic tracker shell and toward a clearer product: landing-page copy, session pagination, login and site-detail flows, public API documentation, OpenAPI schema, and comparison pages for teams looking beyond generic analytics tools.

## What changed in the recent commits

- Rebranded the product surface to airader.
- Extended tracker-session pagination so longer session lists can be inspected instead of stopping at the first page.
- Added public API documentation and an OpenAPI schema.
- Improved the landing page, SEO copy, metrics state, HUD state, and notification-hook state.
- Added Hotjar and Lucky Orange comparison pages that explain the session-replay angle and API/export positioning.

## Why it matters

Session replay gets more useful when it is not trapped inside a closed dashboard. The product direction here is explicit: observe the journey, keep the raw session context reachable, and make the data usable for the customer’s own analysis workflow.

## Public boundary

This public entry describes the product surface and API/documentation work. It does not expose private customer sessions, internal keys, deployment secrets, or analytics data.
